text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. When developers review each other’s code, they can catch bugs early, before the code goes into production. This saves time and effort later. Beyond finding mistakes, reviews are a great way for team members to share knowledge. Junior developers learn from more experienced colleagues, while senior developers gain fresh perspectives. This process helps improve overall team quality by encouraging consistent coding standards and collaboration. Regular code reviews build trust and make the entire team responsible for delivering better software. In short, they are not just about checking for errors—they help everyone grow and improve together.
